description: IRIB (Seda va Sima) holds a constitutional monopoly on all radio and television broadcasting in Iran, directly controlled by the [Office of the Supreme Leader](/orgs/office-of-the-supreme-leader). Its director is appointed by the Supreme Leader. During the Khatami era it was controlled by [Ali Larijani](/people/ali-larijani) (1994–2004) as an explicit hardline counterweight to the reformist press. IRIB has conducted systematic 'Confession TV' programmes broadcasting forced confessions by political prisoners and journalists. IRIB also publishes the daily newspaper *Jam-e Jam*.
